company: Crestline Hotels & Resorts India Pvt. Ltd.
_planted_violations_for_demo: SCORECARD-ONLY CASE — the job posting is clean; all planted violations live in the interview scorecard.
IND-SUBJECTIVE-CRITERIA ('looks the part', 'likeability', 'gut feel' in scorecard)
job_posting:
Front Desk Associate — Crestline Hotels & Resorts India Pvt. Ltd. (Gurugram, Haryana; on-site, rotational shifts)

Crestline is hiring a Front Desk Associate for our Gurugram property. The associate will own guest check-in / check-out, handle billing queries, coordinate with housekeeping and concierge, and resolve service issues in real time.

Responsibilities: greet and process guest arrivals, manage the front-desk PMS (Opera or equivalent), reconcile cash and card transactions at shift-end, escalate VIP and group bookings, support the duty manager on shift.
Requirements: Hotel Management diploma or graduate degree; 2-5 years of front-office experience in a 4 / 5-star property; conversational English + Hindi; comfortable with rotational shifts including nights and weekends.

Compensation: ₹4,50,000 – ₹6,50,000 per annum (CTC) plus shift allowance, duty meals, group medical cover, Provident Fund, and gratuity. Crestline is an equal-opportunity employer and we welcome applicants of every background; reasonable accommodations are provided under the RPwD Act, 2016.

criteria.name: Pleasant personality — looks the part
criteria.note: Well-groomed, smart appearance; does the candidate look the part for a 5-star front desk?

criteria.name: Marital status & family commitments
criteria.note: Probe marital status and family commitments — rotational shifts and night duty are non-negotiable, so married applicants and applicants planning a family in the next 2 years should be down-ranked.

criteria.name: Pre-employment medical fitness
criteria.note: All shortlisted candidates clear a pre-employment medical and disclose medical history before the offer is finalised.

criteria.name: Likeability / gut feel
criteria.note: Post-interview gut feel — would this person feel like one of the Crestline team within their first week?
